name: RETROPERITONEAL LEIOMYOSARCOMA

demographics.Frequency: 2nd most common (28%) primary retroperitoneal malignancy (after liposarcoma)
demographics.Path: necrosis + cystic degeneration + hemorrhage in large tumor; calcifications uncommon
demographics.Location: between diaphragm + renal veins, may extend along entire length of IVC + into

dominant_finding: lobulated mass often > 10 cm in size
distribution: between diaphragm + renal veins, may extend along entire length of IVC + into
discriminating_features: large soft-tissue mass with displacement of
well-defined fat plane between mass and kidney
lobulated mass often > 10 cm in size

general: large soft-tissue mass with displacement of
well-defined fat plane between mass and kidney
US: solid mass isoechoic to liver / rarely hyperechoic
complex mass with cystic spaces + irregular walls
CT: lobulated mass often > 10 cm in size
large cystic areas of tumor necrosis in center of mass
areas of high attenuation ± fluid-debris level with recent hemorrhage
MR: intermediate intensity on T1WI with low-intensity areas of necrosis
inhomogeneous intermediate to high SI on T2WI ← high water content of cystic areas
ANGIO: hypervascular tumor with blood supply from lumbar, celiac, mesenteric, renal arteries
avascular center surrounded by thick hypervascular rind
solid / necrotic extraluminal mass not originating from a retroperitoneal organ with
